Environmental Impact Statement, Dust Abatement at Canyon Ferry Lake

The Bureau of Reclamation proposed a dust abatement project at Canyon Ferry Lake in Montana to address severe dust storms caused by exposed dry soil during low water levels. The plan includes constructing dikes to create ponds, dredging fine materials, and flooding these areas to develop wildlife habitats. This project aims to improve air quality, enhance wildlife habitats, and benefit the local economy by reducing dust pollution and creating a new migratory waterfowl area. The project is expected to start in 1972 and will have minimal impact on the reservoir's storage capacity.
Canyon Ferry Lake, Broadwater and Lewis and Clark Counties, Montana
Broadwater County Board of County Commissioners

The dust abatement project at Canyon Ferry Lake was proposed in response to severe dust storms that were affecting air quality and local health due to exposed dry soil during periods of low water levels. The Bureau of Reclamation aimed to mitigate these issues while also enhancing wildlife habitats and supporting the local economy. There was some public interest in the project, particularly regarding its potential benefits for wildlife and air quality. Ultimately, the project was implemented as planned, contributing to improved conditions in the area.
"The people of the nearby town of Townsend have complained of dust getting into their homes and reducing visibility." — Environmental Impacts of Proposed Action

"We are pleased that something will be done to alleviate this dust problem and wish to assure you that the City Council will do anything possible to help in the effort." — City of Townsend, Office of the Clerk
